claim_chain: Tau filaments from AD brains are hyperphosphorylated full-length tau
Recombinant tau assembly previously required cofactors and yielded different structures
Twelve phosphomimetic mutations induce full-length tau assembly into PHFs without cofactors
NMR data suggest phosphorylation disrupts intramolecular interaction between IVYK motifs
Hyperphosphorylation may be sufficient for formation of the Alzheimer tau fold
PAD12 tau provides a useful tool for studying neurodegeneration mechanisms

typical_pattern: State known background or previous finding
Present new finding or observation from current study
State broader implications or future directions

likely_priorities: Structural verification by cryo-EM that filaments are authentic PHFs
Demonstration that findings represent minimal necessary conditions
Mechanistic explanation for how phosphorylation induces assembly
likely_attacks: Other phosphomimetic combinations might also work but were not tested systematically
Each construct may require its own optimization of assembly conditions
The role of the fuzzy coat in disease mechanisms remains poorly understood
Unknown whether physiological phosphorylation ever reaches levels required for filament formation
The amino-terminal interaction in the paperclip model remains uncharacterized
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly states 'We cannot exclude the possibility that each construct requires its own optimisation of assembly conditions and that constructs with modifications other than those of PAD12 could be made to form PHFs too.' It acknowledges that 'The definition of a minimal set of phosphomimetic mutations that is necessary for the spontaneous assembly of full-length tau into PHFs would require the systematic removal of PAD12 mutations.' The authors note 'The role of tau's fuzzy coat in the molecular mechanisms of disease remains poorly understood' and 'the corresponding interaction between the amino-terminal domain of tau and the core-forming region remains unknown.' They also state 'It remains to be seen if tau filaments form occasionally as a result of this physiological regulation.'

examples.quoted_phrase: These findings show for the first time the nucleation-dependent assembly of full-length recombinant tau into PHFs
Our findings suggest that hyperphosphorylation of tau may be sufficient for the formation of the Alzheimer tau fold
Phosphorylation of the fuzzy coat may facilitate spontaneous filament assembly by reducing the entropic cost of nucleating new filaments
We cannot exclude the possibility that each construct requires its own optimisation of assembly conditions
PAD12 tau will be a useful tool for the study of molecular mechanisms of neurodegeneration

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, author names, or invented numbers were included. The profile is based solely on the provided abstract, discussion, and figure legends.
